title: DSDS Criterion Definition
description: Shared definitions for testable success criteria and links to external standards. Criteria are the test-definition layer of DSDS docs. Each one pairs a stable identifier with a statement you can verify objectively. Tools and agents check implementations against the docs and report pass or fail for each criterion. Used by guideline entries and accessibility blocks. Observed results go in a guideline entry's `evidence`, not here.

reference.description: A citation of an external standard or requirement. This is a published rule the docs address (WCAG, platform guidelines, vendor compliance frameworks).

url.description: URL to the external standard or requirement (e.g., 'https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum').

label.description: A display label for the citation (e.g., '1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)', 'WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size'). When omitted, tools MAY build a label from the URL.

criterion.description: A testable success criterion: a condition an implementation must meet that you can verify objectively. Criteria let docs act as a test harness. A check runs against the `statement` and reports pass or fail under the criterion's `identifier`. Pair it with `techniques` (how to pass), `failures` (what failing looks like), `references` (the external standards it aligns with), and `examples` (usually a passing and a failing case).

identifier.description: Stable identifier for this criterion, unique within the parent entity (e.g., 'touch-target-minimum', 'single-primary-action'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case. Test runs report pass or fail against this identifier. It SHOULD NOT change once checks depend on it. Renaming breaks result history across runs.

title.description: A short display name for the criterion (e.g., 'Touch Target Minimum'). Tools SHOULD render it in docs and test reports beside the identifier.

statement.description: The testable statement of what success looks like. You MUST be able to verify it objectively — by inspection, static analysis, or runtime measurement. Avoid vague language like 'feels right' or 'looks balanced' (e.g., 'Interactive elements present a hit area of at least 44×44 px').

techniques.description: Ways to satisfy this criterion — the passing patterns (e.g., 'Apply `min-height: 44px` to every interactive element', 'Use the `<Button>` component without overriding its default height').

failures.description: Known failure modes — conditions that count as failing this criterion (e.g., 'Setting `height` below 44px on an interactive element', 'Removing `aria-label` from an icon-only button').

references.description: External standards this criterion aligns with or derives from (e.g., a matching WCAG success criterion, a platform guideline).

examples.description: Examples that show the criterion — usually a before/after pair: one passing and one failing implementation.

tags.description: Freeform keywords that link criteria across guidelines and entities (e.g., 'a11y', 'contrast', 'touch-target').
